feature: Move logs from settings.json to dedicated file-based storage
workflow_rationale: This introduces a new Rust module (logs.rs) with Tauri commands, modifies lib.rs for registration, and refactors the frontend hook — a multi-file feature implementation following an established storage extraction pattern.

phases.name: Backend: Create logs.rs module and register in lib.rs
phases.description: Create the new logs.rs Rust module with file-based storage for log entries (single logs.json array file) and register it in lib.rs with legacy cleanup.

subtasks.description: Create src-tauri/src/logs.rs with AppLog struct, get_logs_path() helper, list_logs/save_log/clear_logs Tauri commands, and cleanup_legacy_logs function. The module stores logs as a JSON array in app_data_dir()/logs/logs.json with a 500-entry cap enforced at write time. Follows the transcriptions.rs pattern for directory helpers, error handling, and Store-based legacy cleanup. Uses #[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")] on the AppLog struct. Handles corrupt files gracefully (return empty array, log warning).

verification.expected: Compiles without errors (warning about unused module is OK at this stage)

subtasks.notes: Already implemented. logs.rs exists with all required components and is registered in lib.rs. cargo check fails due to unrelated whisper-rs-sys/CUDA dependency.

subtasks.description: Register the logs module and commands in lib.rs: (1) Add 'mod logs;' declaration at the top with other modules (around line 6). (2) Add logs::list_logs, logs::save_log, logs::clear_logs to the invoke_handler (after the transcriptions commands around line 1211). (3) Call logs::cleanup_legacy_logs() in the setup block after the existing cleanup_legacy_transcriptions call (around line 1221).
subtasks.notes: Already implemented. lib.rs already has: mod logs; declaration (line 6), logs::list_logs/save_log/clear_logs in invoke_handler (lines 1213-1215), and logs::cleanup_legacy_logs() in setup block (line 1227). No code changes needed. cargo check confirms no Rust compilation errors (only unrelated whisper-rs-sys path length issue in deep worktree).

phases.name: Frontend: Refactor useAppLogs hook to use invoke()
phases.description: Refactor the useAppLogs hook to use invoke() for persistence instead of Tauri Store, while preserving the real-time event listener for instant log display.

subtasks.description: Refactor src/hooks/useAppLogs.ts: (1) Remove 'import { Store } from "@tauri-apps/plugin-store"' and all Store.load('settings.json') references. (2) Add 'import { invoke } from "@tauri-apps/api/core"'. (3) Change loadLogs() to call invoke<AppLog[]>('list_logs'). (4) In the app-log event listener, after adding the log to state, fire-and-forget invoke('save_log', { log }) without awaiting. (5) Change clearLogs() to await invoke('clear_logs') then setLogs([]). (6) Remove the frontend MAX_LOGS constant and .slice(0, MAX_LOGS) trimming — the 500-entry limit is now enforced by the backend. (7) Remove the saveLogs helper function entirely. (8) Remove the LOGS_KEY constant.

verification.expected: TypeScript compilation and Vite build succeed with no errors

subtasks.notes: Refactored useAppLogs.ts: removed Store import/usage, LOGS_KEY, MAX_LOGS, saveLogs helper. Now uses invoke() for list_logs, save_log, clear_logs. TypeScript and Vite build pass.

subtasks.description: Verify logs-tab.tsx needs no changes. Line 68 already shows '{logs.length >= 500 && " (limite atteinte)"}' which matches the backend MAX_LOGS=500. Confirm no adjustments are needed and mark complete.
subtasks.notes: Verified: logs-tab.tsx line 68 already has `logs.length >= 500` matching backend MAX_LOGS=500. No changes needed.

phases.name: Integration: Full build verification and code review
phases.description: Verify that both cargo check and pnpm build pass cleanly, and perform a code review to ensure all changes follow established patterns and no regressions were introduced.

subtasks.description: Run cargo check in src-tauri/ and pnpm build at root to verify both backend and frontend compile cleanly with no errors.

verification.command: cd src-tauri && cargo check && cd .. && pnpm build
verification.expected: Both commands succeed with exit code 0

subtasks.description: End-to-end code review: (1) Verify logs.rs creates logs directory and logs.json file correctly. (2) Verify useAppLogs.ts no longer imports Store. (3) Verify lib.rs has all three log commands registered and cleanup_legacy_logs called in setup. (4) Verify the app-log event listener is preserved in useAppLogs.ts. (5) Verify pattern compliance with transcriptions.rs conventions.

verification.instructions: Review all modified/created files: (1) logs.rs follows transcriptions.rs patterns (get_*_dir, Result<_, String>, StoreBuilder for cleanup). (2) lib.rs has module declaration + commands + cleanup registered correctly. (3) useAppLogs.ts uses invoke() not Store, event listener preserved. (4) No regressions in existing imports/functionality. (5) Log entry schema matches: {id, timestamp, level, message}.

parallelism.speedup_estimate: No parallelism — frontend depends on backend commands existing first

acceptance_criteria: cargo check passes in src-tauri/ with no errors
pnpm build succeeds with no TypeScript errors
logs.rs follows transcriptions.rs patterns (get_*_dir, Result<_, String>, StoreBuilder cleanup)
useAppLogs.ts uses invoke() instead of Store for all persistence
app-log event listener is preserved for real-time display
cleanup_legacy_logs registered in lib.rs setup block

verification_steps.name: Frontend Build Check
verification_steps.expected_outcome: TypeScript compilation and Vite build succeed

verification_strategy.reasoning: Low-risk storage refactor for non-critical diagnostic data. Follows an established, proven pattern in the codebase. No security-sensitive data, no external API changes, no auth modifications. Compilation checks are sufficient validation.
